title: Sorting Array of Integers with Incorrect Sort Order

statement.markdown: Write a function that implements the bubble sort algorithm to sort an array of integers in ascending order. However, the implementation contains a bug, causing the array to be sorted in descending order instead. Fix the bug to get the array sorted in ascending order.
statement.outputFormat: The sorted array in ascending order
statement.notes: The input array will contain only positive integers

examples.explanation: Sort the array in ascending order

hints.text: Check the logic of the bubble sort algorithm

editorial.title: Bubble Sort Algorithm
editorial.intuition: The bubble sort algorithm repeatedly steps through the list, compares adjacent elements and swaps them if they are in the wrong order. The pass through the list is repeated until the list is sorted.
editorial.bruteForce: The brute force approach is to generate all permutations of the array and check if they are sorted
editorial.betterSolution: Use a more efficient sorting algorithm like quicksort or mergesort
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use the bubble sort algorithm with the correct logic
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness is that the bubble sort algorithm correctly sorts the array in ascending order
editorial.dryRun: Dry run the bubble sort algorithm on a small array

starterCode.python: def solve(nums): # TODO: implement bubble sort algorithm
starterCode.javascript: function solve(nums) { // TODO: implement bubble sort algorithm }

buggyStarterCode.cpp: bool solve(vector<int>& nums) { for (int i = 0; i < nums.size(); i++) { for (int j = 0; j < nums.size() - i - 1; j++) { if (nums[j] > nums[j + 1]) swap(nums[j], nums[j + 1]); } } return true; }
buggyStarterCode.java: public boolean solve(int[] nums) { for (int i = 0; i < nums.length; i++) { for (int j = 0; j < nums.length - i - 1; j++) { if (nums[j] > nums[j + 1]) { int temp = nums[j]; nums[j] = nums[j + 1]; nums[j + 1] = temp; } } } return true; }
buggyStarterCode.python: def solve(nums): for i in range(len(nums)): for j in range(len(nums) - i - 1): if nums[j] > nums[j + 1]: nums[j], nums[j + 1] = nums[j + 1], nums[j] return True
buggyStarterCode.javascript: function solve(nums) { for (var i = 0; i < nums.length; i++) { for (var j = 0; j < nums.length - i - 1; j++) { if (nums[j] > nums[j + 1]) { var temp = nums[j]; nums[j] = nums[j + 1]; nums[j + 1] = temp; } } } return true; }

solutions.python:
class Solution: def solve(self, nums: list[int]) -> bool: for i in range(len(nums)): for j in range(len(nums) - i - 1): if nums[j] > nums[j + 1]: nums[j], nums[j + 1] = nums[j + 1], nums[j] return True

# Read input from sys.stdin
input_str = sys.stdin.readline().strip() n = int(input_str)
sample_nums = list(map(int, sys.stdin.readline().strip().split()))
solution = Solution()
result = solution.solve(sample_nums)
if result:
    print(*sample_nums, sep=" ")
